context.projectOverview: agent-bober: a multi-agent TypeScript/ESM CLI harness that builds apps via a plan->curate->generate->evaluate pipeline. This plan (spec-20260605-scale-safe-history-memory) hardens the append-only event log against unbounded growth (Layer 1) and later adds a deterministic LLM-free lessons memory the planner reads (Layer 2). Sprint 1 is Layer 1, storage-layer ONLY.

contract.title: Bounded history reads + crash-safe rotation (loadRecentHistory + archive)
contract.description: Make agent-bober's append-only event log scale without breaking its existing programmatic consumers. Add a loadRecentHistory(projectRoot, { limit }) bounded tail reader and crash-safe rotation that moves the oldest entries to .bober/history.archive.jsonl once active history.jsonl exceeds a new history.maxActiveLines config field. loadHistory keeps its full-read contract by reading archive then active concatenated, so resume-cursor reconstruction and the EngineConformanceHarness observe an unchanged full stream. This is storage-layer only: no lessons store, no distillation, no CLI.

successCriteria.description: loadRecentHistory(projectRoot, { limit }) reads only active history.jsonl and returns at most `limit` entries newest-last; a unit test asserts the count cap and that no read is issued against history.archive.jsonl.

Rotation moves the oldest entries to .bober/history.archive.jsonl once active history.jsonl exceeds history.maxActiveLines, leaving at most maxActiveLines entries active; a unit test seeds maxActiveLines+50 entries and asserts active holds at most maxActiveLines and the archive holds the overflow.

loadHistory still returns every entry by reading archive then active concatenated; a unit test writes entries spanning a rotation and asserts loadHistory returns the full ordered set identical to the pre-rotation sequence.

Rotation neither drops nor duplicates entries when interrupted: a unit test simulating a crash between archive-append and active-truncate asserts the active+archive union contains every original entry exactly once.

history.maxActiveLines is a validated zod config field (positive integer, default 2000) in src/config/schema.ts; a schema test asserts the default applies on an empty config and a non-positive value is rejected.
npm run typecheck and npm run build exit 0 and the existing src/state and conformance suites stay green with no new failures beyond the documented flaky tool-count baseline.

nonGoals: Do not change the exported signatures of loadHistory or appendHistory.
Do not alter resume-cursor or conformance logic beyond what regression tests need; their observable output must stay identical.
Do not migrate or rewrite the existing 272-line history.jsonl; rotation applies to new appends going forward.
Do not add any LLM, network, lessons-store, or CLI code — this sprint is the storage layer only.
stopConditions: Stop when loadRecentHistory, crash-safe rotation, and the maxActiveLines config exist, loadHistory still returns the full active+archive stream, and typecheck, build, and tests are green.
Stop when changes are confined to src/state/history.ts (and an optional src/state/history-rotation.ts), src/config/schema.ts, and their collocated tests.
contract.definitionOfDone: History reads scale without breaking existing consumers: rotation keeps the active file bounded by history.maxActiveLines, loadRecentHistory offers a bounded tail read, and loadHistory still returns every entry by concatenating archive+active so resume-cursor and the conformance harness observe an unchanged full history. Rotation is crash-safe with no lost or duplicated entries, and typecheck, build, and the state/conformance tests pass.
assumptions: resume-cursor.ts already discards loadHistory's return value (resume-cursor.ts:19), so bounding reads cannot alter the reconstructed ResumeCursor.
conformance.ts deep-compares the full normalized history (conformance.ts:102,131), so loadHistory must keep returning the complete active+archive stream.
contract.generatorNotes: Edit src/state/history.ts (or add src/state/history-rotation.ts and import it). Add archivePath(projectRoot) = .bober/history.archive.jsonl alongside historyPath. Implement rotation inside appendHistory (or a rotateIfNeeded helper called after append): count active lines; when > maxActiveLines, take the oldest (count - maxActiveLines) lines, append them to the archive file, then atomically rewrite active with the remaining tail using a temp-file + rename so an interruption never loses entries (append-to-archive first, then rename-new-active; on crash before rename, the worst case is duplicate-in-archive which the union test tolerates if you de-dup, OR keep archive-append idempotent by writing the new-active temp first then appending archive then renaming — pick one and document the ordering so C4 holds). Add loadRecentHistory(projectRoot, { limit }) that reads ONLY active history.jsonl, parses with the existing HistoryEntrySchema.safeParse, and returns the last `limit` entries. Update loadHistory to read archivePath (if present) then historyPath, concatenating archive-then-active, preserving existing skip-malformed-line behavior. Add `maxActiveLines: z.number().int().positive().default(2000)` to a history section in src/config/schema.ts (mirror PipelineSectionSchema style) and thread it to rotation (read from config, fall back to default 2000). Use import type for type-only imports; ESM .js specifiers; Unicode box-drawing section headers. Conventional commit: bober(sprint-1): bounded history reads + crash-safe rotation.
contract.evaluatorNotes: Verify loadRecentHistory returns at most `limit` and does not touch the archive (spy on readFile path or assert via a temp project with a populated archive). Verify rotation leaves active <= maxActiveLines and the archive holds the overflow. Verify loadHistory over a rotated store equals the pre-rotation ordered sequence (write N entries, force rotation, assert loadHistory deep-equals the original ordered list ignoring nothing). Verify the crash-safety test: simulate interruption and assert the active+archive union has every entry exactly once. Verify the zod default (parse {} history -> maxActiveLines === 2000) and rejection of 0/-1. Run npm run typecheck (exit 0), npm run build (exit 0), and the existing src/state + conformance tests; tolerate only the documented flaky tool-count baseline. Confirm git diff is confined to history.ts/history-rotation.ts, config schema, and collocated tests — no changes to resume-cursor.ts or conformance.ts logic.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
